attractions.name: 1 Hotel South Beach (1 Hotel Official Site)
attractions.description: 1 Hotel South Beach is a luxury, sustainable sanctuary set along a pristine 600-foot stretch of shoreline in Miami Beach. The oceanfront retreat features four swimming pools, including an adult-exclusive rooftop pool, and is designed using locally sourced and repurposed materials. Guests can enjoy organic, garden-to-table cuisine across multiple on-site dining venues and access holistic wellness treatments at the Bamford Wellness Spa (1 Hotel Official Site).
attractions.address: 2341 Collins Avenue, Miami Beach, FL 33139 (1 Hotel Official Site)
attractions.hours: Hotel Operations: Open 24/7. Specific Outlet Hours: Watr Rooftop Restaurant: Sun–Thurs 11am–11pm, Fri–Sat 11am–1am; AVIV Restaurant: Sun–Thurs 5:30pm–10pm, Fri–Sat 5:30pm–11pm; Pools & Beach: Daily 9:30am–5pm; Bamford Wellness Spa: Daily 9am–8pm (1 Hotel Official Site, 1 Hotel FAQ).
attractions.admission: Free for hotel guests; day passes for non-guests (e.g., Beach Pass) start at $149 (ResortPass). A mandatory resort fee covers amenities like beach chairs and umbrellas (1 Hotel Official Site).
highlights: 'Private 600-foot stretch of white sand beach with complimentary chairs and umbrellas for guests ([1 Hotel Official Site).', 'Four distinct outdoor swimming pools, featuring a 18th-floor rooftop pool with panoramic ocean views (1 Hotel Official Site).', 'Eco-conscious design including in-room water filtration, organic mattresses, and reclaimed wood materials (1 Hotel Official Site).', 'Holistic wellness experiences at the Bamford Wellness Spa and state-of-the-art Anatomy fitness center (1 Hotel Official Site).', 'Complimentary Audi e-tron house car service for local drop-offs within a 3-mile radius (1 Hotel Official Site).']
attractions.tips: Reserve pool daybeds or beach chairs in advance through the hotel concierge, especially during peak weekend hours. Take advantage of the '1 Less Thing' program by leaving unwanted clothing for donation to the Miami Rescue Mission. Visit the 18th-floor rooftop bar, Watr, for sunset views, but note it is adult-only after dark (1 Hotel Official Site, 1 Hotel FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Lincoln Road Mall, Miami Beach Botanical Garden, The Bass Museum of Art, and the historic Art Deco District on Ocean Drive (1 Hotel Official Site, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Art Deco Historic District (Miami Beach Architectural District)
attractions.description: The Art Deco Historic District in Miami Beach contains the world's largest concentration of Art Deco architecture, with over 800 preserved buildings dating from the 1920s to the 1940s. This vibrant square-mile area is characterized by its iconic pastel-colored facades, geometric patterns, neon signage, and unique nautical elements like porthole windows and curved corners. It is a living, walkable museum that serves as a cultural and social hub for the city. (Miami and Beaches, MDPL)
attractions.address: 1001 Ocean Drive, Miami Beach, FL 33139
attractions.hours: Art Deco Welcome Center & Museum: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Daily (MDPL); District: Accessible 24/7 (Miami and Beaches)
attractions.admission: District exploration is free; Art Deco Museum: $7.00 General Admission, Free for Students, Military, and Miami-Dade Residents with ID (MDPL); Official Walking Tours: Approximately $35.00 - $40.00 per person (Viator)
highlights: "World's largest concentration of Art Deco architecture with over 800 historic buildings ([Miami and Beaches)", 'Iconic Ocean Drive stretch between 5th and 15th Streets, famous for its neon-lit hotels like the Colony and Breakwater (Miami and Beaches)', 'The Art Deco Welcome Center and Museum providing educational exhibits and guided walking tours (MDPL)', 'Annual Art Deco Weekend festival celebrating architecture, history, and the arts every January (Art Deco Weekend)', 'Notable architectural styles including Mediterranean Revival and Miami Modern (MiMo) alongside Art Deco (MDPL)', "Historic Lummus Park, offering beachfront views and access to the district's iconic skyline (See Sight Tours)"]
attractions.tips: Visit in the early evening to see the iconic neon signs illuminate the buildings along Ocean Drive. Take an official walking tour led by the Miami Design Preservation League for deep historical insights into the architecture and preservation movement. Don't forget to explore the building lobbies, many of which feature original terrazzo floors and decorative details. (Miami Culinary Tours, Experience Miami Beach)
attractions.nearby: Lummus Park, Ocean Drive, Lincoln Road Mall, The Wolfsonian-FIU Museum, Jewish Museum of Florida-FIU, World Erotic Art Museum (Tripadvisor, Miami and Beaches)

attractions.name: Bay of Pigs Brigade 2506 Museum & Library
attractions.description: The Brigade 2506 Museum and Library is dedicated to the history and legacy of the Bay of Pigs Invasion and the Cuban freedom fighters who participated in the 1961 military operation. The museum serves as both an educational center and a veterans' hub, showcasing authentic artifacts and offering first-hand accounts from surviving members of the assault brigade. (Official Site, Miami and Beaches)
attractions.address: 1821 SW 9th Street (Official Headquarters and New Museum site) and 1338 SW 8th Street (Temporary location)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Sunday: Closed (Tripadvisor, Which Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Time Out Miami, Which Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations encouraged) (Local 10, WPLG)
highlights: 'Authentic artifacts and memorabilia from the Bay of Pigs Invasion ([On the Grid)', 'Original Brigade 2506 flag held by President John F. Kennedy at the Orange Bowl in 1962 (Miami and Beaches)', 'Educational video presentation detailing the invasion events (Miami and Beaches)', 'Interactive exhibits and enlarged historical photographs (Miami Herald)', 'Direct interaction with surviving veterans who serve as museum staff (On the Grid)', 'Comprehensive library focusing on Cuban-American history (WPLG Local 10)']
attractions.tips: Plan to spend 1-2 hours to fully explore the exhibits and engage with the veterans. Visit on weekdays to ensure you can speak with surviving brigade members who often staff the facility. Combine your visit with a walk down Cuban Memorial Boulevard to see the Eternal Flame monument. (Yelp, On the Grid, RoadsideAmerica)
attractions.nearby: Bay of Pigs Monument (Eternal Flame) on Cuban Memorial Boulevard, Maximo Gomez Park (Domino Park), Tower Theater, Ball & Chain, and the various landmarks of Calle Ocho in Little Havana (Tripadvisor, RoadsideAmerica)

attractions.name: Big Cypress National Preserve
attractions.description: Established in 1974, Big Cypress National Preserve protects 729,000 acres of a vast swamp ecosystem in South Florida that is vital to the health of the neighboring Everglades. It features a unique blend of tropical and temperate plant communities and provides a critical habitat for diverse wildlife, including the endangered Florida panther and the American alligator. National Park Service
attractions.address: 33100 Tamiami Trail East
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily National Park Service (Visitor Centers typically 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, closed Dec 25 Florida National Parks Association)
attractions.admission: Free (no entrance fee; charges for camping and off-road vehicle permits apply) National Park Service
highlights: 'Scenic Drives (Loop Road and Turner River Road) [National Park Service', 'Ranger-guided discovery hikes and canoe programs National Park Service', 'International Dark Sky Park astronomy nights National Park Service', 'Wildlife viewing at Oasis and Nathaniel P. Reed Visitor Centers Chrissa Travels FL', 'Bird watching and alligator spotting at H.P. Williams Roadside Park Chrissa Travels FL', 'Off-road vehicle trails for swamp buggies National Park Service']
attractions.tips: Be prepared for changing weather conditions and limited cellular access in remote areas. Use the official NPS app for navigation and check at visitor centers for current ranger-led activities. Always let someone know your itinerary before heading into the backcountry. National Park Service
attractions.nearby: Everglades National Park, Fakahatchee Strand Preserve State Park, Florida Panther National Wildlife Refuge, Ochopee Post Office, Skunk Ape Research Center Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Biltmore Hotel Miami Coral Gables (The Biltmore Hotel)
attractions.category: Hotel, Landmark, Resort (The Biltmore Hotel, City of Coral Gables)
attractions.description: The Biltmore Hotel is a palatial National Historic Landmark built in 1926, featuring spectacular Mediterranean architecture with Italian, Moorish, and Spanish influences. Spanning over 150 acres, this iconic resort has hosted world leaders and celebrities and is renowned for its spiraling Giralda Tower and one of the largest hotel pools in the United States (The Biltmore Hotel, Wikipedia)

attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours. (Note: Pool is open 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily) (The Biltmore Hotel, Travel Weekly)
attractions.admission: Free for historic tours and lobby visits; room rates for guests typically range from approximately $269 to $2,000+ per night depending on the season and suite type (Tripadvisor, Travel Weekly)
highlights: "National Historic Landmark featuring a tower inspired by Seville's Giralda ([Wikipedia)", 'One of the largest hotel pools in the Continental U.S., featuring private cabanas (The Biltmore Hotel)', 'Donald Ross 18-hole, 71-par championship golf course originally designed in 1925 (City of Coral Gables)', 'World-class amenities including a European spa, ten lighted tennis courts, and a modern fitness center (Biltmore Hotel Miami Coral Gables)', 'Renowned dining options including the Italian-inspired Fontana and a celebrated Sunday Champagne Brunch (The Biltmore Hotel)', 'Rich history having served as a hospital during WWII and a campus for the University of Miami medical school (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Pre-register for the free historic walking tours held every Sunday at 2 PM to learn about the hotel's architecture and heritage. Don't miss the internationally acclaimed Sunday Champagne Brunch, though reservations are highly recommended (Dade Heritage Trust, The Biltmore Hotel)
attractions.nearby: Venetian Pool, Miracle Mile shopping district, Village of Merrick Park, Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, and the University of Miami (Tripadvisor, Biltmore Hotel Miami Coral Gables)

attractions.name: Charles Deering Estate
attractions.description: The Deering Estate is a 444-acre historic house museum, cultural and ecological field station, and national landmark located on the coast of Biscayne Bay. It preserves the 1920s era estate of Charles Deering, featuring historic buildings like the Stone House and Richmond Cottage, along with diverse ecosystems and 10,000 years of human history. Deering Estate
attractions.address: 16701 SW 72nd Avenue
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Last admission and gate closure at 4:00 PM) Deering Estate
attractions.days_open: Open daily except for Thanksgiving and Christmas Day Deering Estate
attractions.admission: Adults (15+): $18; Youth (4-14): $10; Seniors (62+): $16; U.S. Military (Active & Veterans): $16; Children (3 & under): Free. (Prices are plus tax) Deering Estate
highlights: ["Stone House: A grand Mediterranean Revival-style mansion built in 1922 to house Charles Deering's art collection.", 'Richmond Cottage: A historic pioneer home and former hotel dating back to 1896.', 'Boat Basin: A scenic waterfront area lined with royal palms, frequently visited by manatees.', 'Mangrove Boardwalk: A trail offering a peaceful walk through a unique coastal ecosystem.', 'Cutler Burial Mound: A centuries-old Tequesta Indian site accessible via guided naturalist tours.']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online via Park Link for convenience. Wear comfortable shoes for trails, bring binoculars for wildlife spotting, and check the website for restoration updates regarding the Stone House. Deering Estate

attractions.name: City of Coral Gables
attractions.description: Coral Gables, famously known as \"The City Beautiful,\" is a premier Mediterranean-themed planned community located in Miami-Dade County. Developed by George Merrick in the 1920s, the city is renowned for its lush tree-lined avenues, historic Mediterranean Revival architecture, and iconic landmarks like the Biltmore Hotel and the Venetian Pool. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 405 Biltmore Way
attractions.hours: City Hall: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Visitor Center: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM Greater Miami & Miami Beach, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Daily (City); Monday - Saturday (Visitor Center/City Hall) Greater Miami & Miami Beach
attractions.admission: Free (City); Venetian Pool: $18-$21; Coral Gables Museum: $12; Fairchild Garden: $24.95 City of Coral Gables, Coral Gables Museum, Fairchild Garden
highlights: ['Venetian Pool: A historic aquatic facility carved from a coral rock quarry featuring waterfalls and caves.', 'Biltmore Hotel: A legendary 1926 luxury landmark with a massive pool and world-class golf course.', "Miracle Mile: The city's central hub for upscale shopping, boutique stores, and international dining.", 'Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden: An 83-acre oasis featuring extensive collections of rare tropical plants.', "Coral Gables Museum: A cultural institution dedicated to the city's history, architecture, and urban design."]

attractions.name: Clevelander South Beach Hotel & Bar
attractions.description: The Clevelander South Beach is an iconic hotel and bar located on Ocean Drive in the heart of the Art Deco District. Known as a staple of the Miami party scene, it features three bars, a pool patio with non-stop entertainment, and daily happy hours. This adult playground offers a vibrant atmosphere with live DJs, dancing, and a variety of signature cocktails and food options.

attractions.hours: Mon - Wed: 11 AM - 2 AM; Thu: 11 AM - 4 AM; Fri - Sat: 11 AM - 5 AM; Sun: 11 AM - 2 AM

highlights: ['Iconic Art Deco architecture and prime location in the heart of South Beach.', 'Non-stop entertainment including pool parties, live DJs, and Rockstar Karaoke.', 'Features three distinct bars and the Clevelander Beach Club experience.', 'Daily Happy Hour specials and 50% off breakfast items before 1 PM.', 'VIP table service and various party packages for bachelor and bachelorette groups.', 'Exclusive 25% discount on food and drinks for Miami-Dade County residents.']

For locals, don't forget to bring proof of residency to enjoy the 25% discount. Dressing stylishly is recommended for evening events and night parties.

attractions.name: Collins Avenue (State Road A1A)
attractions.description: Collins Avenue is a premier thoroughfare in Miami Beach, famously known as the 'Capital of Cool' for its upscale shopping, iconic Art Deco architecture, and vibrant atmosphere. Stretching parallel to the Atlantic Ocean, it serves as a central hub for luxury hotels, trendy boutiques, and high-energy nightlife. The avenue is named after John S. Collins, the developer who built the first bridge connecting Miami Beach to the mainland.

attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Street); Shopping and dining typically 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM or later
attractions.admission: Free to visit (public thoroughfare)
highlights: ['Home to the Collins Avenue/Ocean Drive Shopping District with over 30 designer brands.', 'Features the Art Deco Historic District with hundreds of preserved 1930s-era buildings.', 'Stretches through iconic neighborhoods like South Beach, Surfside, and Bal Harbour.', 'Lined with legendary luxury hotels including the Fontainebleau and the Eden Roc.', 'Direct access to the Miami Beach Boardwalk and stunning Atlantic Ocean views.', 'A primary route for the Miami Beach Trolley, providing easy public transportation.']
attractions.tips: Use the free Miami Beach Trolley (Collins Express) to navigate the long stretch without a car. For the best shopping deals, focus on the area between 5th and 10th streets, and visit during the weekday mornings to avoid the heavy evening crowds. Enjoy the Art Deco architecture by taking a self-guided walking tour starting from the 1000 block.
attractions.nearby: Ocean Drive, Miami Beach Boardwalk, Lincoln Road Mall, Lummus Park, The Bass Museum of Art

attractions.name: Coral Castle Museum Official Website
attractions.category: Museum, Landmark, Sculpture Garden Greater Miami & Miami Beach
attractions.description: The Coral Castle Museum is a mysterious architectural marvel built single-handedly by Latvian immigrant Edward Leedskalnin between 1923 and 1951. Using more than 1,100 tons of coral rock, Leedskalnin constructed the site as a monument to his "Sweet Sixteen" after a broken engagement, claiming to have used the secrets of the ancient Egyptians to move the massive stones. Official Website, Visit Florida
attractions.address: 28655 S. Dixie Highway, Homestead, FL 33033 Official Website
attractions.hours: Open daily from 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Last tour begins at 6:00 PM; Gates close at 6:30 PM Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults: $25, Children (ages 5-12): $10, Children (4 and younger): FREE Official Website
highlights: 'Three-ton triangular coral rock directional gate that rotates 360 degrees [Official Website', 'Hand-carved Polaris telescope made of stone Tripadvisor', "The world's only stone Sundial with seasons Tripadvisor", 'Ed’s self-portrait sculpture and collection of handmade tools Official Website', 'Heart-shaped stone dining table and functioning stone rocking chairs Jen on a Jet Plane', 'The 1923 Admission 10-cent Sign from the original Florida City location Official Website']
attractions.tips: Admission includes a 30-minute documentary and a professionally led public tour, which is highly recommended for understanding the site's history. Tickets are purchased on-site at the gift shop upon arrival. Visitors should manage expectations regarding the property's size, as it is an intimate stone residence rather than a large fortress. Official Website, Jen on a Jet Plane

attractions.name: Española Way
attractions.description: Española Way is a historic pedestrian-only street in Miami Beach, originally developed in the 1920s as an artists' colony modeled after Mediterranean villages in Spain and France. This charming enclave features pink stucco buildings, wrought-iron balconies, and string lights, offering a vibrant "Old World" atmosphere for dining and shopping. (Official Website, Experience Miami Beach)

attractions.tips: Visit in the evening to experience the best atmosphere when the street is most lively and illuminated. Reservations are recommended for popular restaurants, and many establishments offer happy hour specials between 5:00 PM and 7:00 PM. (Feelin Fancy, Official Website)

Established in 1947 for its biodiversity, it is a World Heritage Site, International Biosphere Reserve, and Wetland of International Importance, home to rare species like the Florida panther and American crocodile NPS.

It is celebrated for its lush tropical landscape, bohemian atmosphere, and historic architecture, offering a blend of upscale shopping, bayside dining, and rich cultural heritage. Coconut Grove Miami, Greater Miami & Miami Beach
attractions.address: 3250 Mary Street, Ste #305 (Business Improvement District Office)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; BID Office: Monday–Friday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Yelp, Coconut Grove Miami
attractions.admission: Free (Public Neighborhood) Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Vizcaya Museum and Gardens [Vizcaya Museum & Gardens', 'The Barnacle Historic State Park Florida State Parks', 'CocoWalk Shopping and Dining Center CocoWalk', 'Peacock Park and Waterfront Greater Miami & Miami Beach', 'Dinner Key Marina City of Miami', "Historic 'Little Bahamas' Neighborhood Greater Miami & Miami Beach"]
attractions.tips: Visit the Coconut Grove Farmers Market on Saturdays for organic produce and local crafts. Keep an eye out for wild peacocks roaming the residential streets, a local neighborhood icon. Use the Miami Metrorail (Coconut Grove or Douglas Road stations) to avoid traffic and parking challenges. Tripadvisor, Greater Miami & Miami Beach

attractions.name: Coral Gables Merrick House
attractions.description: The Coral Gables Merrick House is a historic landmark restored to its 1925 appearance, serving as the childhood home of city founder George Merrick. This 14-room residence is set on 1.3 acres of lush grounds featuring heritage gardens and tropical flora. It offers a glimpse into the Roaring Twenties and the early history of the "City Beautiful." (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 907 Coral Way
attractions.hours: Saturday and Sunday: Tours at 1:00 PM, 2:00 PM, and 3:00 PM; Monday–Friday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $5; Seniors & Military: $3; Students (over 12): $3; Students (under 12): $1; Children (under 6): Free (Official Website)
highlights: ['Docent-guided tours of the 14-room historic residence', '1.3 acres of lush tropical grounds and Heritage Garden', 'Historic grotto filled with ten species of fish', 'Expansive wraparound porch perfect for photography', 'Restored interior reflecting its 1920s appearance', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1973']
attractions.tips: Tours are cash only and no reservations are required for individuals. Free parking is conveniently located behind the house, and public restrooms are available on-site. Please note that pets, food, open drinks, and strollers are not permitted inside the house. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Venetian Pool, Biltmore Hotel, Coral Gables Museum, Miracle Mile, Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden (Visit Florida, Official Website)

attractions.description: Dinner Key Marina is Florida's largest wet slip marine facility, featuring 587 slips in a scenic, park-like setting. Originally a base for Pan American Airways' flying boats, the marina is steeped in history and located adjacent to Miami's historic City Hall. City of Miami, US Harbors

highlights: ["Florida's largest wet slip marina with 587 slips and 225 moorings.", 'Located in the heart of historic Coconut Grove on Biscayne Bay.', "Historic site of Pan American Airways' Clipper seaplane base.", 'Adjacent to the iconic Miami City Hall building.', 'Features 24-hour staff, security, and pump-out boat services.', "Proximity to waterfront dining and entertainment at Monty's Raw Bar."]

attractions.name: Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden (fairchildgarden.org)
attractions.description: Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden is an 83-acre tropical paradise established in 1938, featuring extensive collections of rare tropical plants, palms, and cycads. It serves as a museum, laboratory, and conservation research facility dedicated to preserving biodiversity and sharing the joy of tropical gardening (fairchildgarden.org).
attractions.address: 10901 Old Cutler Road, Coral Gables, FL 33156 (fairchildgarden.org)
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (fairchildgarden.org)
attractions.admission: General Admission: Adults $24.95, Seniors (65+) $17.95, Children (3-11) $11.95, Children (2 and under) Free. Flex Tickets: Adults $29.95, Seniors $22.95, Children (3-11) $16.95. Discounts: $9 off for College Students (18-24), $2 off for AAA members, Free for Active Military/Veterans (ftbg.ticketapp.org)
highlights: 'Wings of the Tropics: An exotic butterfly exhibit featuring species from around the world ([fairchildgarden.org)', 'Richard H. Simons Rainforest: The only tropical rainforest in the continental U.S., featuring a 500-foot waterfall (fairchildgarden.org)', 'Rare Plant Conservatory: A showcase of palms, bromeliads, orchids, and ferns (Time Out Miami)', 'Tropical Flowering Trees: A collection of over 740 species, including the famous fragrant cannonball tree (Wikipedia)', 'Whitman Tropical Fruit Pavilion: An exhibit dedicated to tropical fruits from around the globe (fairchildgarden.org)']
attractions.tips: Take the free 90-minute narrated tram tour to see the grounds comfortably. Purchase tickets online or at self-service kiosks to save $5 per ticket compared to in-person prices. Strollers are not permitted inside the butterfly conservatory (fairchildgarden.org) (ftbg.ticketapp.org).

attractions.name: Lowe Art Museum
attractions.description: The Lowe Art Museum, established in 1950 as South Florida's first art museum, serves as a major cultural institution for the University of Miami and the surrounding region. It houses a diverse collection of over 19,000 objects spanning 5,000 years of human creativity across various cultures and time periods. (University of Miami, Wikipedia)

attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Sunday - Tuesday: Closed (Official Website)

Barton Collection of Native American art', 'Ancient Greek and Roman antiquities in the Marchman Gallery', 'Extensive Arts of Asia and Arts of Africa collections', 'Outdoor sculpture garden on the University of Miami campus']
attractions.tips: Advance timed tickets are highly recommended for guaranteed entry; parking is available at the nearby Pavia Garage via the PayByPhone app; exhibition galleries begin closing 15 minutes prior to the museum's official closing time. (Official Website)

attractions.name: Venetian Pool
attractions.description: The Venetian Pool is a historic public swimming pool carved from a coral rock quarry in 1923 and opened in 1924 as the Venetian Casino. It is the only swimming pool included in the National Register of Historic Places and features Venetian-style architecture, stone bridges, and loggias. The pool is fed by 820,000 gallons of fresh spring water from an underground aquifer and is refilled daily. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 2701 De Soto Boulevard, Coral Gables, FL 33134 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Friday: 11:00 am - 5:30 pm; Saturday: 10:30 am - 4:30 pm; Sunday: 10:00 am - 4:30 pm; Monday - Tuesday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Non-Residents: Adults (13+) $18, Children (3-12) $12. Coral Gables Residents: Adults (13+) $7, Children (3-12) $6 (Official Website)
highlights: 'Fed by fresh spring water from an underground aquifer ([Official Website)', 'Features two scenic waterfalls and cave-like grottos for exploration (Official Website)', 'Only swimming pool listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Official Website)', 'Stunning Venetian-style architecture with stone bridges and coral rock formations (Official Website)', 'Refilled daily with over 820,000 gallons of water (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Children must be at least 3 years old and 38 inches tall to enter. Advance ticket booking is highly recommended as capacity is limited and the facility often sells out. Outside food is permitted, but coolers, glass containers, and alcohol are strictly prohibited. Remember to bring your own towel, and note that limited lounge chair rentals are available on-site. (Official Website, FAQ)

attractions.name: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)
attractions.category: Museum, Historic Landmark, and Gardens (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)
attractions.description: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens is a historic estate and National Historic Landmark that serves as a vibrant cultural hub in the heart of Miami. Originally the winter home of agricultural industrialist James Deering, the estate features a Mediterranean-style Main House with original furnishings, ten acres of Italian Renaissance-style formal gardens, and a historic village (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens).
attractions.address: 3251 South Miami Avenue, Miami, FL 33129 (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)
attractions.hours: 9:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. (Last entry at 4:30 p.m.; Main House closes at 5:00 p.m., Gardens close at 5:30 p.m.) (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Monday (Closed on Tuesdays, Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day) (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)
attractions.admission: Adult (13+): $20 (Reduced from $25 due to restoration), Child (6-12): $10, Children (5 and under): Free, Members: Free. Access programs for seniors and others may offer free/discounted entry onsite (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens).
highlights: 'The Main House, a Mediterranean-style villa featuring 34 rooms with original furnishings and art ([Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)', 'Ten acres of formal Italian Renaissance-style gardens overlooking Biscayne Bay (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)', 'The historic Vizcaya Village, which includes farm buildings and the weekly Farmers Market (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens)', 'An extensive collection of European antiquities and decorative arts dating from the 15th through 19th centuries (Freehand Hotels)', "The stone 'Barge,' a sculpted breakwater in the bay that serves as a unique architectural landmark (Wikipedia)"]
attractions.tips: Tickets must be reserved online in advance. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the estate has uneven historic surfaces. An audio tour is available for free via a mobile app or browser with admission (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens). Check for special 'Vizcaya Late' events for evening access (Vizcaya Museum & Gardens).

attractions.name: Bill Baggs Cape Florida State Park
attractions.description: Bill Baggs Cape Florida State Park occupies the southern third of Key Biscayne and is home to the historic 1825 Cape Florida Lighthouse, the oldest standing structure in South Florida. Visitors can enjoy a mile of sandy Atlantic beachfront, frequently ranked among the top ten in the nation, alongside activities like biking, kayaking, and shoreline fishing. The park also serves as a significant site on the National Underground Railroad Network to Freedom, commemorating the journey of escaped slaves to the Bahamas. Florida State Parks, Greater Miami & Miami Beach
attractions.address: 1200 S. Crandon Blvd.
attractions.hours: 8 a.m. to sunset, 365 days a year. (Lighthouse tours: 10 a.m. and 1 p.m., Thursday-Monday). Florida State Parks
attractions.admission: $8 per vehicle (2-8 people), $4 single-occupant vehicle or motorcycle, $2 pedestrians/bicyclists/extra passengers. Overnight boat anchoring: $20 per night. Florida State Parks, WhichMuseum
highlights: ['Climb the 109 spiral steps of the historic 1825 Cape Florida Lighthouse for panoramic ocean views.', 'Relax on over a mile of natural sandy beach, often ranked as one of the best in the U.S.', "Explore the park's 1.5-mile paved bike path winding through native vegetation.", 'Learn about the Underground Railroad Network to Freedom at a designated heritage site.', 'Enjoy some of the best shoreline fishing in the region along the seawall on Biscayne Bay.', 'Spot migratory birds at the Cape Florida Banding Station during spring and fall.']
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends and holidays as the park often reaches capacity and closes. Lighthouse tours require visitors to be at least 42 inches tall. Bring a refillable water bottle for the hydration stations located at the park. Florida State Parks
attractions.nearby: Crandon Park, Miami Seaquarium, Stiltsville, Vizcaya Museum and Gardens. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Crandon Park
attractions.description: Crandon Park is a premier 808-acre urban park located on the northern part of Key Biscayne, offering a blend of natural beauty and recreation. It features a world-class beach, championship golf and tennis facilities, and a dedicated nature center. The park is renowned for its diverse ecosystems, including coastal dunes, mangroves, and seagrass beds.
attractions.address: 6747 Crandon Boulevard, Key Biscayne, FL 33149
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset, Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset, Friday: Sunrise - Sunset, Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset, Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset. Miami-Dade County
attractions.admission: Parking fee: $7 on weekdays, $10 on weekends. Beach and Nature Center admission is Free. Miami-Dade County, A Place Under The Palms
highlights: ['Two-mile scenic beach with shallow, calm waters protected by an offshore sandbar.', 'Crandon Golf at Key Biscayne, a championship 18-hole course set among mangroves and lagoons.', 'Marjory Stoneman Douglas Biscayne Nature Center, offering hands-on exhibits and eco-tours.', 'Crandon Park Tennis Center, which features 27 courts and was the long-time home of the Miami Open.', 'Crandon Marina, providing boat ramps, wet slips, and access to Biscayne Bay.', 'Unique fossilized mangrove reef and extensive nature trails for birdwatching and hiking.']
attractions.tips: Note that flotation devices are strictly prohibited on the beach for safety reasons. Personal kayaks and paddleboards must be launched from the Crandon Marina rather than the beach. Arrive early on weekends and holidays as parking lots fill up quickly. Don't miss the chance to explore the rare fossilized mangrove reef at the north end of the park during low tide.

attractions.description: Historic Virginia Key Beach Park is a scenic 82.5-acre barrier island retreat known for its rich cultural history and natural beauty. Established in 1945 as Miami's only beach for African Americans during the segregation era, it now serves as a multi-cultural landmark featuring restored historic amenities and diverse coastal ecosystems (Official Website, Greater Miami & Miami Beach).

highlights: 'Historic antique carousel and miniature train rides ([Official Website)', 'Extensive mountain bike trails for all skill levels (City of Miami)', 'Largest mangrove wetland in the state (Greater Miami & Miami Beach)', 'Coastal hammock trails and nature boardwalk (Greater Miami & Miami Beach)', 'Rental beachfront cabins with old-Miami charm (Greater Miami & Miami Beach)', 'Water activities including kayaking and paddleboarding (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visit on weekdays for lower admission fees and smaller crowds. Wear a helmet if using the bike trails, as they are mandatory. Bring cash for the entrance fee as some sources indicate it may be cash-only at times (Tripadvisor, Official Website).

attractions.name: Rickenbacker Causeway
attractions.description: The Rickenbacker Causeway is a 3.6-mile multimodal corridor that connects mainland Miami to the barrier islands of Virginia Key and Key Biscayne. It serves as a vital transportation link and a popular route for bicycling, running, and water sports with scenic views of Biscayne Bay. (Miami-Dade County)
attractions.address: 4299 Rickenbacker Causeway (Toll Plaza address) / 4400 Rickenbacker Causeway (General)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily. Customer service for tolls: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM. (Miami-Dade County)
attractions.admission: 2-Axle Vehicle: $3.25 (SunPass), $6.50 (Toll-by-Plate). Higher rates for additional axles. Resident Annual Plan: $72. Commuter/School Plan: $120. Northbound is free. (Miami-Dade County)
highlights: 'Scenic views of the Miami skyline and Biscayne Bay from the William Powell Bridge. ([Wikipedia)', 'Access to popular recreational areas including Hobie Island Beach and Virginia Key. (Key Biscayne Chamber)', 'The Rickenbacker Trail, a 9-mile path for biking, walking, and jogging. (Key Biscayne Chamber)', 'Key access point for the Village of Key Biscayne and Bill Baggs Cape Florida State Park. (Miami-Dade County)', 'Historical significance as a project completed in 1947 and named after WWI ace Eddie Rickenbacker. (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Tolls are only charged in the southbound direction. Ensure you have an active SunPass to avoid higher Toll-by-Plate fees. The causeway is a very popular spot for cyclists, so drivers should remain vigilant. (Miami-Dade County, Key Biscayne Chamber)

attractions.name: Aventura Mall
attractions.description: Aventura Mall is the largest shopping mall in Florida and the fifth-largest in the United States, featuring a premier mix of over 300 luxury boutiques and global brands. The destination is defined by unique experiences, including a world-class contemporary art collection and diverse international dining options like the Treats Food Hall. Centrally located between Miami and Fort Lauderdale, it serves as a major cultural and social hub for the South Florida community.

attractions.name: Biscayne National Park National Park Service
attractions.description: Located within sight of Miami, Biscayne National Park protects a rare combination of aquamarine waters, emerald islands, and fish-bejeweled coral reefs. It preserves the northernmost Florida Keys and over 10,000 years of human history, from prehistoric tribes to shipwrecks and pioneers. As a park that is 95% water, it is a premier destination for boating, snorkeling, and diving National Park Service, National Geographic.
attractions.address: 9700 SW 328th Street, Sir Lancelot Jones Way National Park Service
attractions.hours: Dante Fascell Visitor Center: 9 AM-5 PM Daily; Convoy Point Grounds: 7 AM-5:30 PM Daily; Navigable Waters: 24 Hours Daily; Adams Key: 9 AM-Sunset Daily National Park Service
attractions.days_open: 7 days a week National Park Service
attractions.admission: Free Entrance; Docking (Boca Chita/Elliott Keys): $25.00/day (Fri-Mon & Holidays); Camping: $35.00/night National Park Service
highlights: 'Snorkeling the Florida Reef, the only living coral barrier reef in the continental U.S. [National Park Service', 'Exploring shipwrecks along the Maritime Heritage Trail National Park Service', 'Kayaking through the peaceful mangrove forests of Jones Lagoon National Park Service', 'Visiting the historic lighthouse on Boca Chita Key Scho & Jo', 'Wildlife watching for manatees, dolphins, and sea turtles National Geographic', 'Camping on remote islands like Elliott Key National Park Service']
attractions.tips: The vast majority of the park is only accessible by boat; if you don't have your own, consider a guided tour from the Visitor Center National Park Service. Parking at Convoy Point can fill up quickly on weekends, so early arrival is recommended Scho & Jo. Use the Recreation.gov app to pay for docking and camping fees, and remember that fresh water is not available on most islands National Park Service, Scho & Jo. Winter months provide the most comfortable weather and fewer insects National Park Service.
